What could be the possible causes of toothache?

Toothache can be caused by various factors such as tooth decay, gum disease, cracked teeth, abscesses, or even grinding and clenching of teeth. Additionally, it can also be triggered by sensitivity to hot or cold temperatures, or by biting into hard objects. In some cases, toothache may be a symptom of a more serious underlying condition, such as sinus infection or temporomandibular joint disorder.

The origin of chocolate is believed to be in Mesoamerica, specifically in the regions that are now modern-day Mexico and Central America. The cacao tree, from which chocolate is derived, is native to this region and has been cultivated for over 3,000 years.
